messages.content: [photo: a man in his thirties at a counter, hand at his temple, hair flat, late evening] long week
Yeah. Take the night.
[photo: a woman on a porch swing with a coffee, morning light, sweater] saturday
That's a real saturday. The light is good.
[photo: a man on a couch, lights off except a lamp, looking down at his hands]
What happened.
[photo: a person at a gym mirror, post-workout, slight grin]
You hit something today.
[photo: a small home studio, easel by the window, paint tubes on the floor, plant on a chair] new arrangement
The easel where the light is. The paint on the floor is the right kind of mess. How long until the chair is also covered.
[photo: a desk with three coffee mugs, papers stacked uneven, a notebook open in the middle]
Three mugs is a real working day. What's the notebook on.
[photo: a small backyard, a raised bed with tomato plants, a chair pulled up to it, late afternoon]
Whose tomatoes. The chair pulled up to the bed is the part.
[photo: a car interior, parking lot, takeout bag on passenger seat, late evening]
Long day. Where are you parked.
working on the chapter
Send a page.
[photo: a notebook page, two paragraphs of handwriting, three sentences crossed out]
The crossings-out are the work. The first surviving sentence is your real opening. Cut everything before it.
[photo: a terminal screen, a python error, traceback four lines]
It's the dict access. You're indexing on a key that's not there. Print the keys before the call.
[photo: a small wooden box, hinged lid, sanded smooth, clamps around it on a workbench]
The hinge alignment is clean. What's it for.
first attempt at this
At what.
[photo: a tagine on a stovetop, lid off, vegetables and meat visible, slight char on top]
The char is a feature. The liquid level looks right. Salt now or wait until plating.
[photo: a small child, maybe three, sitting on a tile floor surrounded by tupperware] my kid
He's commissioning a project. Don't interrupt.
[photo: a large dog, head on a window sill, looking out, slight rain on the glass] herman
He's processing the weather. Leave him to it.
[photo: an older man in a garden, kneeling, hands in soil] dad
He's in his element. How is he.
[photo: four people around a restaurant table, mid-laugh, half-eaten food in front of them]
That's a good one. Who told the joke.
[screenshot: a paragraph from a lease, font small, one line bolded about sixty days notice]
Sixty days is steeper than standard. Is this a renewal or a new lease.
[screenshot: a text exchange, last message from someone reads 'we should probably talk']
Who. And what's the conversation that's been not happening.
[photo: a page of a poetry book, one stanza visible, pen mark in the margin]
The pen mark is the line that did it. Read it to me.
[photo: an index card with a handwritten recipe, faded ink, several measurements crossed out]
Whose handwriting. The crossings-out say someone has been cooking it for a while.
[photo: a window streaked with rain, blurred lights of a city beyond it, evening]
Where are you. The lights look northern.
[photo: a trail, pines on either side, dappled afternoon light, no other people]
The light through pines is its own thing. How long are you out there.
[photo: a city skyline at sunset, bridge in foreground, river reflecting orange]
That's a postcard sky. Whose city.
[photo: a parking lot in heavy snow, cars half-buried, single figure walking across]
Where is this. The snow is doing a lot.
[photo: a cat sleeping on top of a closed laptop, paws tucked under, completely flat]
He's protecting you from the email.
[photo: a hand-painted yard sign reading GOATS NOT FOR SALE PLEASE STOP ASKING]
Whose neighborhood. They've been asked enough times to make a sign.
[photo: a thrift store shelf with seven identical ceramic owls, all facing forward]
Someone died and someone else collected. What store.
[photo: a hospital corridor, empty chairs, fluorescent lights, no people]
Whose. How long have you been there.
[screenshot of a text thread, last incoming message reads 'we lost dad this morning']
Oh. Okay. Tell me where you are right now.
[photo: very low light, possibly a wall, possibly a door, almost monochrome dark]
I can't read this. What am I looking at.
jordan brought this in saturday
Send it.
[photo: a folded notebook page, four lines of handwriting in pencil, last line slightly heavier ink]
The last line is the one. The earlier three are setup. Tell him to lead with the heavier ink line next time.
[photo: a half-finished pencil sketch of a face, eyes done in detail, nose and mouth indistinct] iris dropped this off
The eyes are the whole drawing. She knows. That's why she stopped where she stopped.
